questions.question: The `ip link set` subcommand is used only to display device attributes.
questions.explanation: `ip link set` changes device attributes; display belongs to `ip link show`.
questions.source_section: ip link set - change device attributes
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: In normal operation, `ping` waits two seconds between packets by default.
questions.explanation: The default documented interval is one second between packets.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ip address flush` command flushes protocol addresses selected by criteria.
questions.explanation: That is the command's documented purpose.
questions.source_section: ip address flush - flush protocol addresses
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ip address flush` command runs normally even when you give it no arguments.
questions.explanation: The manual states that it does not run when no arguments are given.
questions.source_section: ip address flush - flush protocol addresses
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ss` command is used to dump socket statistics.
questions.explanation: `ss` is described as a utility for dumping socket statistics.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ping` command sends ICMP ECHO_REQUEST packets to network hosts.
questions.explanation: That is the core purpose stated in the manual page name section.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Only the super-user may set `ping` intervals smaller than 2 ms.
questions.explanation: The manual states that intervals below 2 ms require super-user privileges.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Portable scripts can safely rely on `hostname` always existing and following the exact GNU behavior.
questions.explanation: The manual warns portable scripts not to rely on the command's existence or exact behavior.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ss -a` option displays both listening and non-listening sockets.
questions.explanation: That is the documented behavior of `ss -a`.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ip address show` command is used to look at protocol addresses.
questions.explanation: The man page uses that exact description.
questions.source_section: ip address show - look at protocol addresses
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: In `ip address show`, the `up` selector lists every interface, including non-running ones.
questions.explanation: The `up` selector lists only running interfaces.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ss -6` option displays only IPv4 sockets.
questions.explanation: The `-6` option limits output to IPv6 sockets.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ss -s` option computes its summary only by parsing all socket lists first.
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly says `ss -s` does not parse socket lists to obtain the summary.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ss -p` option shows processes using sockets.
questions.explanation: The manual says `-p` shows processes using sockets.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: When `ping` hits an error other than the no-reply case, it exits with code 0.
questions.explanation: The manual says other errors cause exit code 2.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ping -i` option controls the time between sending packets.
questions.explanation: The `-i` option sets the interval in seconds between sent packets.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: You can force `ping` to use one address family by specifying `-4` or `-6`.
questions.explanation: The manual says `-4` and `-6` explicitly enforce one family.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ping -c` option stops only after receiving a given number of replies, regardless of how many requests it sends.
questions.explanation: The `-c` option stops after sending the specified count of ECHO_REQUEST packets.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: With no arguments, the `hostname` command prints the current host system name.
questions.explanation: The GNU Coreutils manual says `hostname` prints the current host system name when called without arguments.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ip address flush` command is documented as unforgiving and capable of purging all addresses.
questions.explanation: The man page includes a direct warning about this.
questions.source_section: ip address flush - flush protocol addresses
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ping` command works only with IPv4 unless a separate `ping6` binary is used.
questions.explanation: The manual says `ping` works with both IPv4 and IPv6.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: With one argument, the `hostname` command appends the string to the current host name instead of replacing it.
questions.explanation: With one argument, `hostname` sets the current host name to the specified string.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ss -4` option displays only IPv4 sockets.
questions.explanation: That is the documented effect of `-4`.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: In `ip link set`, the `up` and `down` modifiers adjust only logging verbosity and do not change interface state.
questions.explanation: The manual says `up` and `down` change the device state to UP or DOWN.
questions.source_section: ip link set - change device attributes
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Without options, `ss` shows only listening sockets.
questions.explanation: By default `ss` shows open non-listening sockets with established connections.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Setting the host name with `hostname` requires appropriate privileges.
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly says appropriate privileges are required to set the host name.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ss -l` option includes both listening and non-listening sockets.
questions.explanation: `ss -l` restricts output to listening sockets only.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If `ping` receives no reply packets at all, it exits with code 1.
questions.explanation: Exit code 1 is used for the no-reply case.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ping -q` option suppresses the summary lines and prints only per-packet output.
questions.explanation: Quiet mode does the reverse: it shows only the summary lines.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `ip-link` manual warns that combining multiple parameter changes in one `ip link set` call can leave the system in an unpredictable state after a failure.
questions.explanation: The warning says a failed multi-change call can move the system to an unpredictable state.
questions.source_section: ip link set - change device attributes
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.
